questionHtml:
Hemoglobin is a conjugated protein consisting of four polypeptide chains with attached heme groups.

[Source: LAGUNA DESIGN/Science Photo Library via Getty Images.]

Explain the shape of the polypeptide chain at X.
markschemeHtml:
1. alpha helix / α helix
  2. type of secondary structure / second level of protein structure;
  3. maintained by hydrogen bonds;
  4. between C=O group and an N–H group;

meta.title: Explain the shape of the polypeptide chain at X.
breadcrumbLabels: B1.2.8. Pleating and coiling of secondary structure of proteins
